description: The structure of the universe in ancient Turkic thought, traditionally seen as a tripartite system.

concepts.meaning: The Eternal Blue Sky, the supreme deity and creator who governs the cosmic order.
Earth-Water. Represents the sacred natural world and the spirits inhabiting it.
The sacred center of the earth, the holy forest and mountain range that serves as the heart of the Turkic people.
Heaven. The realm where the souls of the virtuous go after death.
Hell or the Lower World. The realm of darkness and the punishment of the wicked.
